# Kevin Astuhuaman

Last updated: 2026-07-14

## Positioning

Kevin Astuhuaman is an AI Product Manager, ex-PayPal in AI/ML observability, and Berkeley Haas MBA in the Bay Area. He has seven years of product experience. His two recruiting lanes are applied AI and agent products, and AI platform or technical product management.

## Experience

### PayPal Checkout

Technical Product Manager MBA Intern | May-August 2025

Defined and prototyped an AI-assisted observability workflow for checkout diagnostics.

- A PayPal Checkout internship prototype reduced issue-detection time by 75% in the project workflow across a diagnostic environment spanning 15+ tools.
- A PayPal Checkout internship auto-triage proof of concept completed triage five times faster in the project workflow.

### Trackly

AI Product Manager and builder | 2025-present

Built and operates a real-time job-search product across web, native apps, CLI, MCP, matching, chat, and voice.

- Chose direct company career pages as the wedge after seeing strong roles appear before aggregators during the MBA recruiting cycle.
- Designed quality controls for stale jobs, duplicate roles, false-zero monitoring failures, classification, and match explanations.
- Shipped one product system across web, iOS, macOS, a public CLI, and an MCP server.
- Built a human-reviewed browser-agent harness that combines Trackly MCP context, ATS-specific form rules, Chrome computer use, and a fresh approval gate before submission.

### UC Berkeley Haas Open Innovation Squad

AI Product Manager, Fujitsu and MoBagel challenge | December 2024-May 2025

Worked on AI product adoption, a high-fidelity Figma prototype, roadmap, business model, and United States go-to-market recommendations for MoBagel.

- Conducted product and competitive research around how non-technical business users adopt AI and machine-learning workflows.
- Contributed feature and UX concepts, a high-fidelity Figma prototype, and a roadmap linking user needs to product capabilities.
- Recommended go-to-market positioning, partnership channels, and usage-based pricing for United States expansion.

### Banco de Credito BCP (part of Credicorp)

Product Analyst -> Product Manager -> Senior Product Manager -> Lead Product Manager | 2018-2024

Progression: Lead Product Manager (1 year), Senior Product Manager (1.5 years), Product Manager (1.5 years), Product Analyst (2 years).

- At Banco de Credito BCP, Kevin's work helped more than 100,000 small businesses access a first loan.
- Kevin designed and launched an online credit-evaluation platform that reduced evaluation time from seven days to one hour and generated $40M in incremental loan disbursements in nine months.
- Kevin redesigned BCP's digital value proposition for small businesses, contributing to 14% year-over-year revenue growth while monthly website traffic grew sixfold to 180,000 in 15 months.
- As Lead Product Manager at BCP, Kevin managed nine product direct reports.
- As Lead Product Manager at BCP, Kevin reached 19.55% market share after gaining 129 basis points in seven months.

## Selected work

- [PayPal AI observability](https://portfolio.kevinastuhuaman.com/projects/paypal-ai-observability/): An agentic observability prototype that turned fragmented checkout diagnostics into one evidence-backed investigation path.
- [Trackly](https://portfolio.kevinastuhuaman.com/projects/trackly/): A job-search system built around direct career-page signal, explainable matching, and dependable delivery across human and agent interfaces.
- [Berkeley x MoBagel](https://portfolio.kevinastuhuaman.com/projects/berkeley-mobagel-ai-gtm/): Product discovery, high-fidelity prototyping, roadmap, business model, and United States go-to-market recommendations for an AI platform.
- [BCP small-business products](https://portfolio.kevinastuhuaman.com/projects/smb-fintech-bcp-credicorp/): Six years of progression across lending, digital channels, growth, and product leadership in a regulated financial institution.

## Public evidence

- As of July 14, 2026, Trackly monitored 3,884 active company career sites across 40 ATS and source types, with 173,864 active jobs in its public inventory. Context: Dated active inventory from the verified Azure production report, not a user-adoption claim. Source: https://usetrackly.app/
- Trackly is available publicly on Apple's App Store. Context: Public distribution artifact for the native product. Source: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/trackly-apply-first/id6758267565
- During his PayPal Checkout internship, Kevin conducted more than 20 stakeholder conversations and mapped a diagnostic environment spanning more than 15 tools. Context: Internship discovery scope, not a PayPal-wide system inventory. Source: https://portfolio.kevinastuhuaman.com/projects/paypal-ai-observability/
- A PayPal Checkout internship prototype reduced issue-detection time by 75% in the project workflow across a diagnostic environment spanning 15+ tools. Context: Prototype-workflow result, not a PayPal-wide production claim. Source: https://portfolio.kevinastuhuaman.com/projects/paypal-ai-observability/
- A PayPal Checkout internship auto-triage proof of concept completed triage five times faster in the project workflow. Context: Proof-of-concept workflow result, not a PayPal-wide production claim. Source: https://portfolio.kevinastuhuaman.com/projects/paypal-ai-observability/
- At Banco de Credito BCP, Kevin's work helped more than 100,000 small businesses access a first loan. Context: Production small-business lending outcome; this claim stays in the BCP experience section, not the portfolio bio. Source: https://portfolio.kevinastuhuaman.com/projects/smb-fintech-bcp-credicorp/
- Kevin designed and launched an online credit-evaluation platform that reduced evaluation time from seven days to one hour and generated $40M in incremental loan disbursements in nine months. Context: Production lending outcome. Source: https://portfolio.kevinastuhuaman.com/projects/smb-fintech-bcp-credicorp/
- Kevin redesigned BCP's digital value proposition for small businesses, contributing to 14% year-over-year revenue growth while monthly website traffic grew sixfold to 180,000 in 15 months. Context: Production digital-channel outcome; 180K is monthly website traffic, never product MAU or a generic user count. Source: https://portfolio.kevinastuhuaman.com/projects/smb-fintech-bcp-credicorp/
- As Lead Product Manager at BCP, Kevin reached 19.55% market share after gaining 129 basis points in seven months. Context: Production portfolio outcome from Kevin's public resume. Source: https://portfolio.kevinastuhuaman.com/projects/smb-fintech-bcp-credicorp/
- As Lead Product Manager at BCP, Kevin managed nine product direct reports. Context: Public leadership-scope claim. Source: https://portfolio.kevinastuhuaman.com/resume/
- Kevin led digital-wallet API partnerships at BCP that grew monthly sales from zero to $20M in under six months. Context: Production partnership outcome from Kevin's public resume. Source: https://portfolio.kevinastuhuaman.com/resume/
- Kevin co-led a BCP go-to-market program that grew monthly digital sales from $24M to $55M in 11 months. Context: Production go-to-market outcome from Kevin's public resume. Source: https://portfolio.kevinastuhuaman.com/resume/
- Trackly ships a public command-line interface and Model Context Protocol server. Context: Public technical distribution artifact; separate from the App Store listing. Source: https://github.com/trackly-app/trackly-cli
- Kevin published Enterprise AI Interface Kit, one interactive operator workflow with seven product contracts for provenance, calibrated confidence, permissions, approval, observable traces, scoped recovery, and honest empty states. Context: An original reference product with fictional data, not employer work, production authorization logic, or a disclosure of Trackly internals. Source: https://kevinastuhuaman.github.io/enterprise-ai-interface-kit/
- Kevin's public AI Product Builder Stack documents 65 verified tools and product patterns across eight layers, with lifecycle states and implementation evidence. Context: A maintained evidence map, not a claim that every tool is current production infrastructure. Source: https://kevinastuhuaman.github.io/ai-product-builder-stack/
- Kevin published Human Control Plane, an interactive product study that maps consequence, reversibility, model uncertainty, and intent freshness to four human-oversight patterns. Context: A deterministic product-design study built with synthetic scenarios, not a production authorization framework. Source: https://kevinastuhuaman.github.io/human-in-the-loop-patterns/
- Kevin published AI Product Motion Studies, three interactive product-design studies with 13 inspectable states for workflow structure, accountable recovery, and cross-surface continuity. Context: Original interfaces with synthetic data and a public motion contract, not employer product work or a disclosure of Trackly internals. Source: https://kevinastuhuaman.github.io/ai-product-motion-studies/
- Berkeley Haas's official past-challenges page lists the Fujitsu and MoBagel AI Business Adoption Acceleration Challenge. Context: Official program context only. Kevin's participation and deliverables are public resume claims. Source: https://haas.berkeley.edu/open-innovation/programs/past-challenges/
- At Berkeley Haas, Kevin co-launched the school's first Tech and AI Summit, which received more than 560 registrations. Context: Public campus leadership claim. Source: https://portfolio.kevinastuhuaman.com/resume/
